title: project.source.remove
description: Remove a source link from an existing project. Permitted when lifecycle in {setup_incomplete, ready, blocked}. Refused with lifecycle.read_only when lifecycle in {prepared, processing, completed, archived}. When removal leaves zero confirmed sources the project transitions back to setup_incomplete (newLifecycle present in response). Removing the last confirmed source is gated by a second confirmation: the caller must retry with confirmLastSource=true or the server returns lifecycle.last_confirmed_source. Ratified: GRILL 2026-05-22 R-Remove. Convention: camelCase (A7 exception for new contracts).

message.description: Human-readable explanation. MUST NOT include secrets or absolute filesystem paths.

details.description: Structured context. lifecycle.read_only MUST include currentLifecycle. lifecycle.last_confirmed_source MUST include remainingConfirmedCount (always 0 in this case).

remainingConfirmedCount.description: Number of confirmed sources that would remain after removal. 0 triggers lifecycle.last_confirmed_source unless confirmLastSource=true.

Request.description: Source removal request. projectSourceId identifies the ProjectSource row (keyed by inventoryId on the project). confirmLastSource must be true when removing the last confirmed source; omitting it returns lifecycle.last_confirmed_source so the caller can surface a confirmation dialog.

projectSourceId.description: The inventoryId of the ProjectSource to remove.

confirmLastSource.description: Must be true when removing the last confirmed source. Omitting or setting false causes lifecycle.last_confirmed_source to be returned, giving the caller a chance to surface a confirmation dialog.

removedSourceId.description: The inventoryId of the removed ProjectSource.

newLifecycle.description: Present when removal triggered a lifecycle transition (e.g. ready → setup_incomplete when last confirmed source was removed).
